approach: Enable ApplicationSet resources in non-control plane namespaces by configuring the ArgoCD CR
evidence: Lines 124-228: Managing the application set resources in non-control plane namespaces
notes: Technology Preview feature. Source: modules/gitops-enabling-the-application-set-resources-in-non-control-plane-namespaces.adoc (PROCEDURE)
section: Managing the application set resources in non-control plane namespaces
why: To support multitenancy and allow teams to manage their own ApplicationSet deployments in isolated namespaces

approach: Set the sourceNamespaces parameter in the applicationSet spec of the ArgoCD CR
evidence: Lines 176-228: Enabling the application set resources in non-control plane namespaces
notes: Requires setting spec.applicationSet.sourceNamespaces. Creates RBAC resources automatically. Source: modules/gitops-enabling-the-application-set-resources-in-non-control-plane-namespaces.adoc (PROCEDURE)
section: Enabling the application set resources in non-control plane namespaces
user_story: Enable ApplicationSet resources in specific namespaces
why: To explicitly define which non-control plane namespaces can create and manage ApplicationSet resources

approach: Use explicit names, glob patterns, or regex patterns in sourceNamespaces field
evidence: Lines 239-253: About configuring ApplicationSet namespaces using names and patterns
notes: Supports explicit names, glob-style wildcards, and regex patterns. Evaluated at reconcile time. Source: modules/gitops-configure-application-set-namespaces-using-names-and-patterns.adoc (CONCEPT)
section: About configuring ApplicationSet namespaces using names and patterns
user_story: Control ApplicationSet permissions using namespace patterns
why: To grant permissions across multiple namespaces efficiently without listing each one individually

approach: Add the namespace name directly to spec.applicationSet.sourceNamespaces
evidence: Lines 264-288: Enable ApplicationSet in a specific namespace
notes: Simplest approach for single namespace. Source: modules/gitops-enable-application-set-in-a-specific-namespace.adoc (PROCEDURE)
section: Enable ApplicationSet in a specific namespace
user_story: Enable ApplicationSet in a specific namespace by name
why: To grant ApplicationSet permissions to a single, well-known namespace

approach: Use glob-style wildcard patterns like team-* in sourceNamespaces
evidence: Lines 299-323: Define glob-style in wildcard patterns
notes: Example: team-* matches team-1, team-2. Source: modules/gitops-define-glob-style-in-wildcard-patterns.adoc (PROCEDURE)
user_story: Grant ApplicationSet permissions using glob wildcards
why: To grant permissions across multiple namespaces that share a common naming convention

approach: Use regex patterns wrapped in forward slashes in sourceNamespaces field
evidence: Lines 334-374: Define regular expressions in patterns
notes: Patterns must be wrapped in /pattern/ format. Requires Apps in Any Namespace to be enabled. Source: modules/gitops-define-regular-expressions-in-patterns.adoc (PROCEDURE)
user_story: Control ApplicationSet access using regular expressions
why: To precisely control which namespaces receive permissions using complex matching rules

approach: Define allowed SCM Provider URLs in spec.applicationSet.scmProviders
evidence: Lines 385-427: Allowing Source Code Manager Providers
notes: SCM Provider and PR generators disabled by default for security. Must explicitly allow URLs. Source: modules/gitops-allowing-scm-providers.adoc (PROCEDURE)
user_story: Secure SCM Provider access for ApplicationSets
why: To prevent secret exfiltration through malicious API endpoints while enabling SCM Provider and PR generators

approach: Enable and configure Progressive Sync with ApplicationSet strategies
evidence: Lines 443-623: Using Progressive Sync in OpenShift GitOps
notes: Technology Preview feature. Manages synchronization across multiple clusters. Source: using-progressive-sync-in-openshift-gitops.adoc (ASSEMBLY)
section: Using Progressive Sync in OpenShift GitOps
why: To orchestrate safe, staged rollouts of changes across multiple clusters with visibility into health and progress

approach: Add extraCommandArgs or set env variable in Argo CD CR to enable Progressive Sync
evidence: Lines 642-744: Enabling Progressive Sync
notes: Two methods: extraCommandArgs with --enable-progressive-syncs, or env variable ARGOCD_APPLICATIONSET_CONTROLLER_ENABLE_PROGRESSIVE_SYNCS. Source: modules/gitops-enabling-progressive-sync.adoc (CONCEPT)
user_story: Enable Progressive Sync feature in GitOps
why: To activate Progressive Sync functionality for ApplicationSet controllers

approach: Add --enable-progressive-syncs to spec.applicationSet.extraCommandArgs in ArgoCD CR
evidence: Lines 660-696: Adding the extraCommandArgs argument to the Argo CD CR
notes: Operator automatically reconciles and updates ApplicationSet controller deployment. Source: modules/gitops-adding-extraCommandArgs-to-argo-cd.adoc (PROCEDURE)
section: Adding the extraCommandArgs argument to the Argo CD CR
user_story: Enable Progressive Sync using command arguments
why: To enable Progressive Sync using the command argument method

approach: Set ARGOCD_APPLICATIONSET_CONTROLLER_ENABLE_PROGRESSIVE_SYNCS=true in spec.applicationSet.env
evidence: Lines 707-744: Setting the env environment variable in the Argo CD CR
notes: Alternative to extraCommandArgs. Operator automatically reconciles. Source: modules/gitops-setting-env-to-argo-cd.adoc (PROCEDURE)
section: Setting the env environment variable in the Argo CD CR
user_story: Enable Progressive Sync using environment variable
why: To enable Progressive Sync using the environment variable method

approach: Review creation and deletion strategy options for ApplicationSets
evidence: Lines 755-773: Understanding ApplicationSet strategies
notes: Two components: creation strategy (type field) and deletion strategy (deletionOrder field). Finalizer prevents removal until all apps deleted. Source: modules/gitops-applicationset-strategies.adoc (CONCEPT)
why: To choose appropriate strategies for controlling application deployment patterns and cleanup sequences

approach: Evaluate AllAtOnce for simultaneous updates vs RollingSync for staged rollouts
evidence: Lines 784-803: Understanding creation strategies
notes: AllAtOnce = default, all apps simultaneously. RollingSync = staged by labels. Apps not matched by any step must be manually synced. Source: modules/gitops-applicationset-creation-strategies.adoc (CONCEPT)
user_story: Choose between AllAtOnce and RollingSync creation
why: To select the creation strategy that matches deployment risk tolerance and dependency requirements

approach: Set spec.strategy.type to AllAtOnce in ApplicationSet
evidence: Lines 815-946: Configuring AllAtOnce creation strategy
notes: Default behavior. Suitable for independent applications without dependencies. Source: modules/gitops-configuring-allatonce-creation-strategy.adoc (PROCEDURE) and modules/gitops-example-allatonce-creation-strategy.adoc (REFERENCE)
why: To update all applications managed by the ApplicationSet at the same time

approach: Set spec.strategy.type to RollingSync and define steps with label selectors
evidence: Lines 957-1144: Configuring RollingSync strategy
notes: Requires labels on generated apps. Steps process sequentially. maxUpdate controls concurrent updates within a step. Progressive Sync panel displays rollout status in UI. Source: modules/gitops-configuring-rollingsync-strategy-enhanced.adoc (PROCEDURE) and modules/gitops-example-rollingsync-progressive-sync.adoc (REFERENCE)
user_story: Deploy applications in stages with RollingSync
why: To control deployment progression through environment stages and reduce risk with staged rollouts

approach: Review AllAtOnce and Reverse deletion strategies
evidence: Lines 1155-1177: Understanding deletion strategies
notes: AllAtOnce = default, all deleted simultaneously. Reverse = delete in reverse order of rollingSync.steps. Useful for dependent services. Source: modules/gitops-applicationset-deletion-strategies.adoc (CONCEPT)
why: To choose appropriate strategy for tearing down applications in the correct order

approach: Set spec.strategy.deletionOrder to AllAtOnce
evidence: Lines 1189-1328: Configuring AllAtOnce deletion strategy
notes: Default deletion behavior. Works with both AllAtOnce and RollingSync creation strategies. Finalizer ensures cleanup before ApplicationSet removed. Source: modules/gitops-configuring-allatonce-deletion-strategy.adoc (PROCEDURE) and modules/gitops-example-allatonce-deletion-strategy.adoc (REFERENCE)
why: To remove all applications at the same time when they don't have deletion dependencies

approach: Set spec.strategy.deletionOrder to Reverse with RollingSync type
evidence: Lines 1339-1490: Configuring ApplicationSet reverse deletion order
notes: Requires type: RollingSync with defined rollingSync.steps. Deletes in reverse order of steps. Source: modules/gitops-configuring-applicationset-reverse-deletion-strategy.adoc (PROCEDURE) and modules/gitops-example-reverse-deletion-order.adoc (REFERENCE)
why: To ensure dependent services are deleted in the correct sequence (e.g., frontend before backend)

approach: Verify Progressive Sync is enabled and ApplicationSet uses RollingSync strategy
evidence: Lines 1501-1539: Troubleshooting Progressive Sync
notes: Common issues: section not appearing or Unknown state. Verify RollingSync strategy and Progressive Sync enabled in ArgoCD CR. Source: modules/gitops-troubleshooting-progressive-sync.adoc (CONCEPT) and modules/gitops-troubleshooting-progressive-sync-section-not-appearing.adoc (PROCEDURE)
why: To diagnose why the Progressive Sync panel is not appearing in the Application details view

approach: Check labels, annotations, namespace location, and ApplicationSet controller logs
evidence: Lines 1550-1558: Troubleshooting when application shows Unknown state
notes: Verify labels/annotations, same namespace as ArgoCD instance, and check controller logs. Source: modules/gitops-troubleshooting-progressive-sync-unknown-state.adoc (CONCEPT)
section: Troubleshooting when application shows Unknown state
user_story: Diagnose Unknown application state in Progressive Sync
why: To resolve Unknown state errors in Progressive Sync status

approach: Use the Progressive Sync status panel in Argo CD Web UI
evidence: Lines 611-623: Overview of enabling Progressive Sync
notes: Displays rollout wave, health, and messages for each application. Available when RollingSync strategy configured. Source: modules/gitops-overview-enabling-progressive-sync.adoc (CONCEPT)
why: To track rollout progress, identify bottlenecks, and ensure compliance with deployment policies
